author: lOrm
content: Added classifier-based auto-allow in src/permissions.rs (#571): auto_allow_score(tool_name, args) -> f32 and PermissionManager::check_auto_allow(tool, args, threshold). Heuristic scorer (Bayesian-style, no ML dep): read-only tools = 1.0; safe bash prefixes (ls/pwd/cat/echo/git status/git diff/git log/...) = 0.95; destructive bash tokens (rm -rf, sudo, dd, mkfs, curl, wget, shutdown, /dev/, chmod 777) = 0.0 hard veto; project-tree edits = 0.6; default = 0.3. check_auto_allow consults explicit Deny rules FIRST so a Deny always beats classifier allow. 4 tests pin: read-only=1.0, safe-bash>=0.9, destructive=0.0, explicit-deny-overrides. NOT included: autoModeState global circuit-breaker, headless AbortError fast-path, acceptEdits would-allow short-circuit — those need PermissionMode lattice work and are left as follow-ups. Pairs with #577 DenialTracker for fallback-to-prompting once thresholds cross.
